task: Remove misleading 'time saved' metric from analytics display and clarify that inferred goals cannot be marked as completed without proper tracking.
discussion_summary: Session focused on auditing the analytics schema and display logic. User identified that 'time saved' (wall-clock session duration) is not a useful metric—it doesn't convey actionable information. Separately, user noted that goals are inferred from checkpoints, making it impossible to claim they're 'completed' without explicit completion tracking. Decision: remove both metrics entirely from the UI for now rather than display misleading data.

accomplishments.what: Removed 'time saved' metric display from askr.py CLI output
Committed and pushed removal of misleading analytics line
Clarified that goals_completed cannot be inferred without explicit tracking mechanism

next_actions.action: Audit askr.py display logic to confirm 'time saved' line is fully removed from today_summary() output and no other references remain
next_actions.why: Ensure the metric removal is complete and won't resurface in any output path

next_actions.action: Design explicit goal completion tracking schema: decide whether goals are marked complete by user input, checkpoint prompt signal, or git commit detection
next_actions.why: Current inference-only approach is unreliable; need a ground-truth mechanism before displaying any completion metrics

next_actions.action: Update roadmap.md Phase 3.11 section to document the JSON handover schema requirements and confirm it aligns with current implementation_state.md tracking
next_actions.why: Roadmap was edited this session; ensure it reflects current state and doesn't contradict active tracking

next_actions.action: Verify context checkpoint cards display correct 'turns remaining' in staging environment (from open goals)
next_actions.why: This was an open goal at session start; needs validation before next phase

decisions.decision: Remove 'time saved' metric entirely from CLI output rather than redefine it
decisions.reason: Wall-clock session duration is not actionable; no redefinition makes it useful. Better to show nothing than misleading data.

decisions.decision: Do not display 'goals completed' until explicit completion tracking is implemented
decisions.reason: Current goals are inferred from checkpoints only; marking them 'completed' without user or system confirmation is unreliable.

user_rejected_decisions.what_was_proposed: Keep 'time saved' metric but redefine it to show something more useful
user_rejected_decisions.user_signal: How would showing the user how much time they spent on Claude even be useful? ... so for now remove it.

user_rejected_decisions.what_was_proposed: Infer goals from checkpoints and mark them as completed
user_rejected_decisions.user_signal: goals done, being the goals inferred majoritily right? so we are going to infer goals and then call them completed? ... so for now remove it.

failed_approaches.approach: Attempted to grep analytics.json for completed_goals or goals_completed fields
failed_approaches.reason: Schema has no such field; only duration_seconds, trigger, and session metadata exist. Confirmed that goal tracking was never implemented.

relational_files.why: Defines the schema for session metrics; removal of 'time saved' display requires understanding what data is actually available
Tracks all modifications and tool runs; updated this session to log the git commit
Phase 3.11 JSON Handover Schema section is active; Phase 4 was removed this session

blockers: No explicit goal completion tracking mechanism exists; cannot implement goals_completed metric until design is finalized
